disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Can swallow water/saliva without choking or drooling; normal voice (against food-bolus impaction/airway compromise)
trigger_question_examples: Can you sip and swallow water without choking, and is your voice normal?
Are you drooling or unable to swallow your own saliva?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can swallow water and my saliva, my voice is normal, and I'm not drooling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever and no acute painful parotid/submandibular swelling (against suppurative sialadenitis)
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, or painful swelling in front of your ear or under your jaw?
Any pus taste in the mouth?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, and no swelling in front of my ear or under my jaw.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms triggered by dry crackers on a background of the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition sicca; relieved partly by lubrication/water
trigger_question_examples: Did this start with dry/crumbly food, and do you have dry eyes/mouth (the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition)?
Does artificial saliva or water help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it started with dry crackers, I have the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition, and water/saliva spray helps a bit.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is food stuck in my throat? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that being able to swallow water and saliva with a normal voice and no drooling makes a food-bolus obstruction very unlikely; the burning is severe dryness friction from the dry-eyes-and-mouth (sicca) condition. Advise artificial saliva before meals, avoid dry foods, and give a bolus/parotitis safety-net — without ambulance over-triage.
